prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Well, dear, a CPU or "central processing unit" is like the brain of a computer. It's responsible for running all the programs and doing all the work that makes a computer actually do things. But, like the human brain, sometimes it gets a bit overwhelmed if you ask it to do too much at once. That's why sometimes the computer gets a bit slow or freezes when you're trying to do a lot of stuff at the same time. It's not that it's broken, dear, it just needs a little break or maybe some help from other parts of the computer.
